sections.paragraph:
ἀφύλακτος, ον (s. φυλακή, φυλάσσω; Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; Sb 6002, 15 [II b.c.]; Jos., Ant. 14, 169; cp. Ezk 7:22; 23:39 A) pert. to absence of security, without guarding (them) of cult images made of ordinary substance as opp. to those kept in temples open to the public Dg 2:7 (v.l. ἀφυλάκτως).—DELG s.v. φύλαξ.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 159
